title: Bundle Overview
tagline: Cite Evidence | Make Inferences | Support Analysis
standard_alignment_content: RI.6.1: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text. Students learn to identify explicit information and make logical inferences supported by evidence.
standard_breakdown_content:
What students must know: Difference between explicit and implicit information, types of textual evidence, inference strategies
What students must do: Locate and cite specific evidence, make logical inferences, analyze text meaning
Required skills: Close reading, critical thinking, text analysis, evidence evaluation
Depth of knowledge: Level 2-3 (analysis and strategic thinking)
whats_included_content:
1. Bundle Overview Page
2. Vocabulary Pack
3. Anchor Reading Passages
4. Reading Comprehension Questions
5. Writing Prompt Pack
6. Graphic Organizer Pack
7. Mini-Lesson & Anchor Chart
8. Exit Tickets
9. Short Quiz
10. Performance Task
11. Homework Page
12. Final Review Page + Answer Key
learning_objectives_content:
• Identify explicit textual evidence from informational texts
• Make logical inferences based on textual clues
• Cite specific evidence to support analysis
• Distinguish between stated and implied information
• Analyze author's purpose using textual support
suggested_pacing_content:
Day 1: Introduce explicit vs. implicit information concepts
Day 2: Practice identifying and citing textual evidence
Day 3: Develop inference skills with guided practice
Day 4: Apply skills to complex informational passages
Day 5: Complete performance task and assessment
materials_needed_content:
• Printed reading passages and worksheets
• Highlighters for marking textual evidence
• Graphic organizers for inference tracking
• Chart paper for anchor charts
• Sticky notes for annotation
• Access to digital texts (optional)
differentiation_tips_content:
For Struggling Readers: Provide sentence frames, use shorter passages, offer partner support during evidence hunting activities.
For Advanced Learners: Challenge with complex texts, require multiple evidence sources, add synthesis tasks across passages.
For ELL Students: Pre-teach key vocabulary, use visual supports, allow native language discussion before English responses.
assessment_overview_content:
Formative: Exit tickets, graphic organizers, peer discussions, teacher observations during guided practice
Summative: Performance task requiring evidence citation and inference analysis
Standards-Based: Rubric measuring evidence quality, inference accuracy, and text analysis depth
